flashcards.front: What is the purpose of onCreate() method?
flashcards.back: Called when the Activity is first created. Used for one-time initialization like setting content view and binding UI components.
When the Activity is partially obscured by another Activity or dialog. It's always called when the Activity is going to the background.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between onStop() and onDestroy()?
flashcards.back: onStop() is called when the Activity is no longer visible. onDestroy() is the final call before the Activity is destroyed and may not be called in low memory situations.
When an Activity is returning to the foreground after being stopped (not paused). It's called before onStart().

flashcards.front: Why should critical data be saved in onPause()?
flashcards.back: Because onPause() is guaranteed to be called before the process might be killed, unlike onStop() or onDestroy().
